latest.title: Auroras glow when solar particles hit air
latest.script_key: auroras start with charged particles from the sun watch the moving color because charged particles follow earth s magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere when they hit oxygen and nitrogen the air releases light the sky is reacting to space weather which rare sky event next

latest.title: Bees taste with their feet not their mouth
latest.script_key: bees taste flowers with their feet watch their front legs tap the petals that s why you never see them lick the pollen like we do they re checking the sugar not collecting it yet what s the first thing you d taste with your hands

reasons: script does not expose enough concrete visual detail
latest.title: Seals track fish trails with whiskers
latest.script_key: seals do spacewalks underwater their flippers aren t feet they re underwater wings that never tire watch the shoulder blades pop up under their skin as they power forward that s why they cover 100 miles a day chasing fish ever seen a seal just glide like that thought it was laziness wrong

latest.title: Monkeys wash food when grit gets annoying
latest.script_key: monkeys can change habits to solve small problems watch the hands near the food because some monkeys learn useful routines from their group washing or handling food differently can remove grit and make eating easier the habit spreads because it works which primate trick should come next

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
latest.script_key: cats use grooming to reset their scent map watch the tongue and paw movements because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat that can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move the cleanup is also a signal which pet behavior should we decode next

latest.title: Dogs read the world through scent maps
latest.script_key: dogs build a map with smell watch the nose before the feet because every sniff samples layers of scent dogs can learn who passed where they moved and what changed since the last check the ground is basically a news feed what should they sniff next

latest.title: Magnets make invisible fields visible
latest.script_key: magnets can show a hidden force map watch the filings line up because each tiny piece becomes a small magnet in the field together they trace the direction of the force around the magnet the pattern is physics drawing itself which force next

latest.title: Rivers don't flow the way maps show
latest.script_key: maps show rivers as straight lines but they don t work like that watch this bend it s not just decoration it s a survival tool that s why the river carves the land fastest where it curves the most ever notice how your favorite fishing spot is always by a bend i d bet on it

latest.title: These rocks fool everyone about erosion
latest.script_key: rocks stay put longer than you d think look close those cracks they re usually softer rock wearing away first not the tough top layer that s why arches form the edges erode leaving the bridge behind did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Glaciers carve mountains faster than rain
latest.script_key: here s the part nobody tells you those rugged peaks ice did this glaciers drag rocks like sandpaper scraping valleys deeper every year because ice holds on tight where water just slips away that s why yosemite s walls are sheer cliffs next time you re hiking up ask yourself could you outlast a glacier

latest.title: Tall trees don't just drink from roots like we thought
latest.script_key: watch the water climb upward not down that s because these tall trees don t just suck from the roots like we learned their bark actually leaks tiny jets of water upward against gravity that s why look close you ll see it s not just sap moving ever notice how leaves stay wet even after the rain stops what s your favorite tree to hug

latest.title: Elephants have the world's strongest itches
latest.script_key: this elephant s scratch lasts 30 seconds because its skin can t reach its own back notice how it uses the tree like a back scratcher friction wears the bark smooth that s why old elephants get these perfect itch stations what would you use to scratch this hard to reach spot

latest.title: Bees dance directions back to the hive
latest.script_key: bees can point the hive toward food watch the body angle because a bee dance can carry direction and distance the hive reads that pattern like a living map then sends more workers to the same flowers tiny body huge navigation system which insect should we decode next

latest.title: Horses remember familiar faces for months
latest.script_key: horses can remember familiar faces watch the eyes and ears because horses can learn who is familiar and who feels safe that memory changes how calmly they approach people and other horses the look is part recognition part decision which herd animal next

latest.title: Plants turn sunlight into stored sugar
latest.script_key: plants turn light into food watch the leaf surface because chlorophyll captures light energy and uses it to build sugar from air and water the green color is a tiny factory at work which plant clue should we decode next

latest.title: Sharks can feel electricity in the water
latest.script_key: sharks can feel electricity in the water watch the head before the turn because sharks have sensory pores that detect tiny electric fields from other animals that helps them hunt when sight is limited the ocean is full of signals we cannot feel would you swim differently

latest.title: Forests can cool the air below them
latest.script_key: forests can cool the air below them watch the shade and mist because leaves block direct sun and release water vapor that combination can make the forest floor feel like a different climate the canopy is a cooling machine not just cover which forest layer next

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next

latest.title: Coral reefs are built by tiny living builders
latest.script_key: coral reefs are built by tiny living builders watch the reef texture because coral animals build hard skeletons that stack into habitat fish algae and countless small creatures then use that structure like a city the color sits on top of engineering which habitat next

latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Storm clouds reveal a storm's heat engine
latest.script_key: storm clouds show where a storm is feeding watch the spiral shape because warm ocean air rises near the center and releases heat as clouds build rotation organizes that energy into bands from above the storm is not random it is an engine which satellite clue next

latest.title: Fossils turn old bones into time clues
latest.script_key: fossils can preserve a clue from deep time watch the shape in the rock because fossils form when buried remains leave mineral records behind the original body is gone but the pattern can reveal age habitat and behavior which ancient clue should we read next

latest.title: Storm clouds show air layers changing over time
latest.script_key: storm clouds show air layers changing over time watch the cloud deck because winds at different heights can push each layer in a different direction a timelapse turns that invisible motion into a map which sky motion should we read next

latest.title: Crystals grow by repeating one tiny pattern
latest.script_key: crystals build shape from repeating atoms watch the edge grow because atoms or molecules lock into a repeating arrangement once the pattern starts new pieces fit most easily along the same order that is why a crystal can look designed which reaction should we slow down next

latest.title: Lightning turns air into a shock wave
latest.script_key: lightning turns air into a shock wave watch the flash first because lightning heats a narrow path of air extremely fast that air expands suddenly and the pressure wave reaches us as thunder the sound is the atmosphere snapping back which storm signal next
